untranslatable_reason: This signal requires fundamental/event-driven data not available in technical indicators: (1) dividend history tracking and omission detection, (2) dividend yield comparison to sector average, (3) earnings trend analysis, (4) dividend confirmation across quarters. The signal is a qualitative risk management rule (adjust valuations and hedging) rather than a quantitative entry/exit signal. It requires external dividend event detection, sector benchmarking data, and fundamental earnings tracking—none of which are present in the allowed technical indicator set.
translation_notes: NATENBERG_DRY_23_0 is a dividend policy risk management principle from options theory, not a mechanical trading signal. The rule advises traders to monitor and adjust option valuations when dividend policy changes are suspected. Entry and exit conditions reference [THRESHOLD_NEEDED] placeholders for dividend yield adjustment, probability cuts, and quarter counts—these require parameterization with fundamental data sources outside the scope of price/volume/technical indicators. This signal is best implemented as a checklist rule within position management logic rather than a backtestable quantitative rule.
